meaning: n.穴居人,隐居者,老顽固,类人猿
examples: "The old music business is a troglodyte-ish system," she said in an interview.
He would be devolved to the lowliest life-form, a mere troglodyte, exiled from his own kind.
When you pick up the helm of seeing you finally understand why the blind troglodyte would find it worthless.
Congress is supposedly changing its troglodyte method of calculating the cost of tax cuts.
Many cave dwellings and fairy-chimney chapels have been converted into boutique hotels, where you can live the troglodyte life in luxury.
